script_text: Machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. For example, instead of identifying a few key variables for facial recognition, machine learning algorithms take in a digital image represented as a rectangular grid of colored pixels. While each pixel in the image offers very little information about a person, the facial features unique to each human arise from the patterns of pixels that result from seeing many images of the same person. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

script_text: We can determine an economy's macroeconomic health by examining a number of goals: growth in the standard of living, low unemployment, and low inflation. How can we use government macroeconomic policy to pursue these goals? A nation's central bank conducts monetary policy, which involves policies that affect bank lending, interest rates, and financial capital markets. For the United States, this is the Federal Reserve. A nation's legislative body determines fiscal policy, which involves government spending and taxes. For the United States, this is the Congress and the executive branch, which originates the federal budget. These are the government's main tools.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: Machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. For example, instead of identifying a few key variables for facial recognition, machine learning algorithms take in a digital image represented as a rectangular grid of colored pixels. While each pixel in the image offers very little information about a person, the facial features unique to each human arise from the patterns of pixels that result from seeing many images of the same person. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

script_text: We can determine an economy's macroeconomic health by examining a number of goals: growth in the standard of living, low unemployment, and low inflation. How can we use government macroeconomic policy to pursue these goals? A nation's central bank conducts monetary policy, which involves policies that affect bank lending, interest rates, and financial capital markets. For the United States, this is the Federal Reserve. A nation's legislative body determines fiscal policy, which involves government spending and taxes. For the United States, this is the Congress and the executive branch, which originates the federal budget. These are the government's main tools.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: Machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. For example, instead of identifying a few key variables for facial recognition, machine learning algorithms take in a digital image represented as a rectangular grid of colored pixels. While each pixel in the image offers very little information about a person, the facial features unique to each human arise from the patterns of pixels that result from seeing many images of the same person. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

A nation's legislative body determines fiscal policy, which involves government spending and taxes. For the United States, this is the Congress and the executive branch, which originates the federal budget. These are the government's main tools.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: Machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. For example, instead of identifying a few key variables for facial recognition, machine learning algorithms take in a digital image represented as a rectangular grid of colored pixels. While each pixel in the image offers very little information about a person, the facial features unique to each human arise from the patterns of pixels that result from seeing many images of the same person. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. script_text: We can determine an economy's macroeconomic health by examining a number of goals: growth in the standard of living, low unemployment, and low inflation. For the United States, this is the Congress and the executive branch, which originates the federal budget. These are the government's main tools.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: Machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. For example, instead of identifying a few key variables for facial recognition, machine learning algorithms take in a digital image represented as a rectangular grid of colored pixels. While each pixel in the image offers very little information about a person, the facial features unique to each human arise from the patterns of pixels that result from seeing many images of the same person. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: Machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. For example, instead of identifying a few key variables for facial recognition, machine learning algorithms take in a digital image represented as a rectangular grid of colored pixels. While each pixel in the image offers very little information about a person, the facial features unique to each human arise from the patterns of pixels that result from seeing many images of the same person. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: Machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. For example, instead of identifying a few key variables for facial recognition, machine learning algorithms take in a digital image represented as a rectangular grid of colored pixels. While each pixel in the image offers very little information about a person, the facial features unique to each human arise from the patterns of pixels that result from seeing many images of the same person. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

For the United States, this is the Congress and the executive branch, which originates the federal budget. These are the government's main tools.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: Machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. For example, instead of identifying a few key variables for facial recognition, machine learning algorithms take in a digital image represented as a rectangular grid of colored pixels. While each pixel in the image offers very little information about a person, the facial features unique to each human arise from the patterns of pixels that result from seeing many images of the same person. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

script_text: We can determine an economy's macroeconomic health by examining a number of goals: growth in the standard of living, low unemployment, and low inflation. How can we use government macroeconomic policy to pursue these goals? A nation's legislative body determines fiscal policy, which involves government spending and taxes. For the United States, this is the Congress and the executive branch, which originates the federal budget. These are the government's main tools.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: Machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: Machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. For example, instead of identifying a few key variables for facial recognition, machine learning algorithms take in a digital image represented as a rectangular grid of colored pixels. While each pixel in the image offers very little information about a person, the facial features unique to each human arise from the patterns of pixels that result from seeing many images of the same person. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: Machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. script_text: Machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. For example, instead of identifying a few key variables for facial recognition, machine learning algorithms take in a digital image represented as a rectangular grid of colored pixels. While each pixel in the image offers very little information about a person, the facial features unique to each human arise from the patterns of pixels that result from seeing many images of the same person. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: Machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. For example, instead of identifying a few key variables for facial recognition, machine learning algorithms take in a digital image represented as a rectangular grid of colored pixels. While each pixel in the image offers very little information about a person, the facial features unique to each human arise from the patterns of pixels that result from seeing many images of the same person. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

script_text: We can determine an economy's macroeconomic health by examining a number of goals: growth in the standard of living, low unemployment, and low inflation. script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: Umm, machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. For example, instead of identifying a few key variables for facial recognition, machine learning algorithms take in a digital image represented as a rectangular grid of colored pixels. While each pixel in the image offers very little information about a person, the facial features unique to each human arise from... the patterns of pixels that result from seeing many images of the same person. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

script_text: We can determine an economy's macroeconomic health by examining a number of goals: growth in the standard of living, low unemployment, and low inflation. How can we use government macroeconomic policy to pursue these goals? A nation's central bank conducts monetary policy, which involves policies that affect bank lending, interest rates, and financial capital markets. For the United States, this is the Federal Reserve. A nation's legislative body determines fiscal policy, which involves government spending and taxes. For the United States, this is the Congress and the executive branch, which originates the federal budget. These are the government's main tools.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: Machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. For example, instead of identifying a few key variables for facial recognition, machine learning algorithms take in a digital image represented as a rectangular grid of colored pixels. While each pixel in the image offers very little information about a person, the facial features unique to each human arise from the patterns of pixels that result from seeing many images of the same person. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: Machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. For example, instead of identifying a few key variables for facial recognition, machine learning algorithms take in a digital image represented as a rectangular grid of colored pixels. While each pixel in the image offers very little information about a person, the facial features unique to each human arise from the patterns of pixels that result from seeing many images of the same person. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: Machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. For example, instead of identifying a few key variables for facial recognition, machine learning algorithms take in a digital image represented as a rectangular grid of colored pixels. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

script_text: Machine learning uses generalized algorithms, with a massive model of the underlying phenomena. For example, instead of identifying a few key variables for facial recognition, machine learning algorithms take in a digital image represented as a rectangular grid of colored pixels. While each pixel in the image offers very little information about a person, the facial features unique to each human arise from the patterns of pixels that result from seeing many images of the same person. Think about the way your phone may look at you when you try to access it using facial recognition. The algorithm is not going to try to match your face to a saved picture of you. Rather, it uses machine learning to extract patterns out of a person's face and match them.

script_text: We can determine an economy's macroeconomic health by examining a number of goals: growth in the standard of living, low unemployment, and low inflation. For the United States, this is the Congress and the executive branch, which originates the federal budget. These are the government's main tools.

script_text: As they recovered from plague, famine, and political conflict, people across many regions during the fourteenth century took the opportunity to rebuild and rebound. Although some empires fell and once-thriving trade routes were abandoned, other entities emerged to take their place and establish the foundations of a truly modern global society. As the crises of the fourteenth century came to an end, geopolitical boundaries shifted, religions expanded into many new areas, and social traditions transformed to meet the needs of an ever-expanding world. Many social and political structures of the fourteenth century, such as the Mongols' dominance and the economic and land-ownership conventions that made up the feudal system, ultimately ceased to exist.

script_text: In eukaryotic cells, the pyruvate molecules produced at the end of glycolysis are transported into mitochondria, which are sites of cellular respiration. If oxygen is available, aerobic respiration will go forward. In mitochondria, pyruvate will be transformed into a two-carbon acetyl group that will be picked up by a carrier compound called coenzyme A, which is made from vitamin B5. The resulting compound is called acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA can be used in a variety of ways by the cell, but its major function is to deliver the acetyl group derived from pyruvate to the next pathway in glucose catabolism.

model_output: The student provides a clear, accurate, and logically structured explanation of the fate of pyruvate leading to the formation of acetyl-CoA, a process often called the link reaction or pyruvate oxidation. The organization is a strong point, moving sequentially from the product of glycolysis (pyruvate) to its transport into the mitochondria, its conversion into an acetyl group, and its combination with coenzyme A. The mention of vitamin B5 as a precursor to coenzyme A is a nice detail that demonstrates knowledge beyond the absolute basics. However, the explanation lacks critical depth by omitting two key products of this reaction: the release of a molecule of carbon dioxide and, most importantly, the reduction of NAD+ to NADH. The production of NADH is a crucial energy-capturing step and its omission leaves out a major purpose of the reaction. An ideal presentation would describe this as an oxidative decarboxylation reaction, accounting for all inputs (pyruvate, NAD+, CoA) and all outputs (acetyl-CoA, CO2, NADH), thereby providing a more complete biochemical picture.  Rating: 5
